task: rotate the pink block towards the right
raw:
SCENE ANALYSIS - A white shelf holds a white tray with a pink block and a yellow block. The pink block is on the tray, adjacent to the yellow block. The tray is a flat surface. STRATEGY - To rotate the pink block right, first grasp it, then rotate the gripper around the block's center while maintaining contact, and finally place it back on the tray. This requires precise control to avoid dropping. AFFORDANCE REASONING - Step 1 reach the pink block at its body for stable contact; Step 2 grasp the block's body for secure hold; Step 3 rotate the gripper around the block's center; Step 4 place the block back on the tray.
Goal: on(pink_block, white_tray); stable(pink_block)
Scene: pink_block, yellow_block, white_tray, shelf
Step 1: reach(pink_block)
  affordance_region: the body of the pink block
  contact: gripper_state(open) [progress]
  spatial: distance(gripper, pink_block, <, 0.03) [completion]
  direction: approach_axis(0, 0, -1) [progress]
  done_when: distance(gripper, pink_block) < 0.03
Step 2: grasp(pink_block)
  affordance_region: the body of the pink block
  contact: holding(pink_block) [completion]
  direction: grasp_axis(0, 0, -1) [progress]
  done_when: holding(pink_block)
Step 3: rotate(gripper -> around pink_block)
  affordance_region: the body of the pink block
  contact: holding(pink_block) [progress]
  direction: rotate_axis(0, 0, 1) [progress]
  done_when: angle(gripper, pink_block) > 45
Step 4: place(pink_block -> white_tray)
  affordance_region: an empty area on the white tray
  contact: surface_contact(pink_block, white_tray) [completion]; released(pink_block) [completion]
  pose: stable(pink_block) [completion]
  done_when: surface_contact(pink_block, white_tray) AND released(pink_block)
<END_OF_PLAN>

steps.affordance_region: the body of the pink block
the body of the pink block
the body of the pink block
an empty area on the white tray
